summary: Frontend-only retest of iter_7 issues. Clipboard fallback WORKS (welcome-copy shows 'Copied ✓', affiliate-copy shows 'Link copied' toast, no uncaught errors). Direct-URL nav protection WORKS (/astrologer/dashboard while logged-out cleanly redirects to /astrologer/login, no path recursion). Astrologer dashboard KPIs render when reached via direct URL (kpi-upcoming, kpi-completed, kpi-total-commission, kpi-pending-commission all visible). Availability add-slot + save works with 'Availability saved' toast. Affiliate link contains '/?ref=<code>'. Logout redirects to /astrologer/login. HOWEVER, the sidebar NavLink navigation is STILL BROKEN: clicking astro-nav-consultations / -availability / -affiliate / -dashboard from within the astrologer workspace triggers the exact same infinite path recursion the iter_7 fix was supposed to resolve (URL becomes /astrologer/dashboard/consultations/dashboard/dashboard/...).

ui_bugs.component: Astrologer.jsx sidebar NavLinks + inner catch-all Route
ui_bugs.issue: CRITICAL — path recursion NOT fixed for sidebar navigation. Under react-router-dom 7.15.0, relative `to` values (`to="dashboard"`, `to="consultations"`, etc.) inside a component rendered by a splat route `/astrologer/*` resolve against the FULL current URL rather than the parent route's pathnameBase. Clicking `astro-nav-consultations` from /astrologer/dashboard produces URL /astrologer/dashboard/consultations, which then never matches any child Route, so `<Route path="*" element={<Navigate to="dashboard" replace />}>` fires and appends `/dashboard` again — infinite loop. Direct URL entry (e.g. window.location or nav('/astrologer/dashboard')) works because that's absolute. The App.js wrapping fix (Route path="/astrologer/*") is necessary but not sufficient — Astrologer.jsx itself must use ABSOLUTE paths. REQUIRED FIX: in /app/frontend/src/pages/Astrologer.jsx: (a) NAV entries -> `to: '/astrologer/dashboard'`, `'/astrologer/consultations'`, `'/astrologer/availability'`, `'/astrologer/affiliate'`; (b) inner Routes: `<Route index element={<Navigate to='/astrologer/dashboard' replace />} />` and `<Route path='*' element={<Navigate to='/astrologer/dashboard' replace />} />`. Alternatively pass `relative='route'` on every NavLink/Navigate, but absolute paths are the cleanest v7-safe fix.

action_items: In /app/frontend/src/pages/Astrologer.jsx replace all relative sidebar NAV entries and inner <Navigate> targets with ABSOLUTE paths (/astrologer/dashboard, /astrologer/consultations, /astrologer/availability, /astrologer/affiliate). This is the only remaining blocker for the astrologer workspace nav flow.
critical_code_review_comments: React Router v7 relative-link semantics differ from v6 inside splat routes — future workspace shells should prefer absolute route paths or explicit `relative='route'` NavLink prop.

AstroDashboard shows KPI cards immediately with no loading skeleton on slow response — mostly fine, but the earlier 'stays on Loading… forever on API failure' remark still applies for a couple of the astrologer sub-pages (AstroAvailability, AstroAffiliate) which use `.catch(() => {})` silently — no error state UI.

success_rate.frontend: 85% — clipboard fixes and direct-nav guard both work; KPI cards render; availability + affiliate flows work when reached by URL; only sidebar NavLink navigation still recurses

context_for_next_testing_agent: After the Astrologer.jsx absolute-path fix lands, re-run the E2E sidebar navigation portion of the test (click astro-nav-consultations, -availability, -affiliate, -dashboard in sequence and assert URL is exactly /astrologer/<page> after each). Clipboard + direct-nav guard + set-password redirect + availability save + affiliate copy + logout are all verified passing and don't need re-testing. Skip backend re-run.
rca of the issue: React Router 7.15 changed how relative `to` resolves when the enclosing route is a splat (`/astrologer/*`). In v6, `to='dashboard'` from a component rendered by such a route resolves against the pathnameBase (/astrologer), yielding /astrologer/dashboard. In v7 the resolution treats the full matched pathname as the base, yielding /astrologer/dashboard/dashboard on the first click, then the catch-all `<Route path='*'>` fires `<Navigate to='dashboard'>` which itself resolves against the newly matched splat, appending `/dashboard` on every render — infinite loop. Fix: use absolute paths in Astrologer.jsx NavLinks and Navigates.
